title: Longitudinal EEG Test-Retest Reliability in Healthy Individuals

funding_references.funder_name: Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación - CODI, Universidad de Antioquia
Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación (CODI), Universidad de Antioquia

description: This longitudinal dataset comprises resting-state EEG recordings from 43 healthy adults collected across four sessions over approximately two years (mean inter-session interval: 7.2 months). The dataset was designed to evaluate the test-retest reliability of quantitative EEG measures using automated preprocessing methods including independent component analysis and wavelet-enhanced artifact removal, enabling assessment of neural component stability and regional power dynamics over time.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during resting-state sessions from 43 healthy participants across four longitudinal timepoints spanning approximately two years. Raw EEG signals were recorded in BIDS-compliant format (.eeg, .vhdr, .vmrk files) with associated metadata. Data processing employed an automated preprocessing pipeline incorporating independent component analysis and wavelet-enhanced artifact removal techniques.
